message:
fix(fcp-raptorq): rewrite dense fallback decoder with overdetermined GF(256) solve and post-hoc verification

Fixes the 6 failing golden erasure recovery tests caused by the
inactivation decoder converging on wrong-but-equation-consistent
solutions for certain K/erasure combinations.

Root cause: the previous dense fallback (marked with "This is WRONG —
missing source data is UNKNOWN, not zero") substituted missing source
identity rows with repair equations one-at-a-time, leaving the system
potentially underdetermined and allowing spurious solutions.

The rewritten try_reconstruct_dense() builds a properly overdetermined
system:
- S + H constraint rows (LDPC + HDPC with zero RHS)
- One identity row per received source symbol
- K' - K padding identity rows (zero RHS)
- One LT equation row per received repair symbol

This provides more rows than columns (L×L intermediate symbols),
eliminating spurious degrees of freedom.  After solving, the result
is verified against ALL received source symbols, ALL received repair
symbols, and all constraint rows — returning InsufficientSymbols on
any mismatch rather than silently producing garbage.

Additional fixes:
- Virtual padding ESI rejection: symbols with ESI in [K..K') are now
  rejected on receipt, preventing silent corruption of the decode.
- Dense fallback is now wired into the main decode path: when the
  inactivation decoder either produces a hash-mismatched result or
  fails with a Recoverable failure class, the code falls back to the
  dense solver.
- verify_payload_hash() extracted into its own method for reuse.
- encode.rs: fix import from fcp_async_core::raptorq to crate::codec.
- golden.rs/lib.rs/oti.rs: unused import cleanup and doc formatting.

Tests:
- decoder_rejects_malformed_symbol_size_without_dense_panic
- decoder_rejects_virtual_padding_esi
- dense_reconstruction_rejects_inconsistent_repair_row

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
